From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British Army officer and colonial administrator (1755–1811)

| [General](/wiki/General_(United_Kingdom) "General (United Kingdom)") [The Right Honourable](/wiki/The_Right_Honourable "The Right Honourable")   Henry Edward Fox | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Born | 4 March 1755 |
| Died | 18 July 1811(1811-07-18) (aged 56) |
| Allegiance | [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") United Kingdom |
| Branch | [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army") |
| Rank | [General](/wiki/General_(United_Kingdom) "General (United Kingdom)") |
| Conflicts | [American Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War") [French Revolutionary Wars](/wiki/French_Revolutionary_Wars "French Revolutionary Wars") [Irish Rebellion of 1803](/wiki/Irish_Rebellion_of_1803 "Irish Rebellion of 1803") |
| Spouse | Marianne Clayton |
| Relations | [Henry Stephen Fox](/wiki/Henry_Stephen_Fox "Henry Stephen Fox") (son) [Henry Fox, 1st Baron Holland](/wiki/Henry_Fox,_1st_Baron_Holland "Henry Fox, 1st Baron Holland") (father) [Lady Caroline Lennox](/wiki/Lady_Caroline_Lennox "Lady Caroline Lennox") (mother) |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/Fox_%28BaronHolland%29_Arms.png/250px-Fox_%28BaronHolland%29_Arms.png)](/wiki/File:Fox_(BaronHolland)_Arms.png)

[Canting arms](/wiki/Canting_arms "Canting arms") of Fox, Baron Holland: *Ermine, on a chevron azure three fox's heads and necks erased or on a canton of the second a fleur-de-lys of the third*

[General](/wiki/General_(United_Kingdom) "General (United Kingdom)") **Henry Edward Fox** (4 March 1755 – 18 July 1811) was a [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army") officer and colonial administrator who served as the [governor of Minorca](/wiki/Governor_of_Minorca "Governor of Minorca") and [governor of Gibraltar](/wiki/Governor_of_Gibraltar "Governor of Gibraltar").

## Family

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henry_Edward_Fox&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Family")]

He was a son of [Henry Fox, 1st Baron Holland](/wiki/Henry_Fox,_1st_Baron_Holland "Henry Fox, 1st Baron Holland") and [Lady Caroline Lennox](/wiki/Lady_Caroline_Lennox "Lady Caroline Lennox") (1723–1774), and a younger brother of the politician [Charles James Fox](/wiki/Charles_James_Fox "Charles James Fox") (1749–1806). His mother's father was [Charles Lennox, 2nd Duke of Richmond](/wiki/Charles_Lennox,_2nd_Duke_of_Richmond "Charles Lennox, 2nd Duke of Richmond") (1701–1750), an illegitimate grandson of King Charles II.

## Life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henry_Edward_Fox&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Life")]

He attended [Westminster School](/wiki/Westminster_School "Westminster School") before being commissioned as a [cornet](/wiki/Cornet_(rank) "Cornet (rank)") in the 1st King's Dragoon Guards in 1770. Soon after that he spent a year's leave at the military academy at Strasbourg. After his return he rose to [lieutenant](/wiki/Lieutenant_(British_Army_and_Royal_Marines) "Lieutenant (British Army and Royal Marines)") (1773) then [captain](/wiki/Captain_(British_Army_and_Royal_Marines) "Captain (British Army and Royal Marines)") (1774).

## American War of Independence

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henry_Edward_Fox&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: American War of Independence")]

In 1773 he moved to the [38th Regiment of Foot](/wiki/38th_Regiment_of_Foot "38th Regiment of Foot"), stationed at [Boston](/wiki/Boston "Boston"), and fought in the [American War of Independence](/wiki/American_War_of_Independence "American War of Independence") (spending 1778–79 on leave in England). By the end of the war he had risen to colonel and king's [aide-de-camp](/wiki/Aide-de-camp "Aide-de-camp"), and he then moved to command the forces in [Nova Scotia](/wiki/Nova_Scotia "Nova Scotia") (1783–89), where he was influential in the creation of the new colony of [New Brunswick](/wiki/New_Brunswick "New Brunswick"), and then the [Chatham Barracks](/wiki/Royal_Marine_Barracks,_Chatham "Royal Marine Barracks, Chatham") (1789–93).

## Later career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henry_Edward_Fox&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Later career")]

Next he was quartermaster-general on the [duke of York](/wiki/Prince_Frederick,_Duke_of_York_and_Albany "Prince Frederick, Duke of York and Albany")'s staff in Flanders to replace the recently killed [James Moncrief](/wiki/James_Moncrief "James Moncrief") (1793–95) and fought in the Netherlands theatre of the [French Revolutionary Wars](/wiki/French_Revolutionary_Wars "French Revolutionary Wars"). He then served as Inspector-General of the recruiting service (1795–99), Colonel of the [10th (North Lincolnshire) Regiment](/wiki/10th_Foot "10th Foot") (1795–1811), Lieutenant-governor of Minorca (1799–1801) following its [capture from the French](/wiki/Capture_of_Minorca_(1798) "Capture of Minorca (1798)"), commander in chief of all British Mediterranean forces outside [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") (1801–03, replacing General Sir [Ralph Abercromby](/wiki/Ralph_Abercromby "Ralph Abercromby") fatally wounded at the [battle of Alexandria](/wiki/Battle_of_Alexandria_(1801) "Battle of Alexandria (1801)")) and finally [Commander-in-Chief](/wiki/Commander-in-Chief "Commander-in-Chief"), Ireland (1803). In Ireland he was caught off-guard by [Robert Emmet](/wiki/Robert_Emmet "Robert Emmet")'s [Dublin uprising](/wiki/Irish_rebellion_of_1803 "Irish rebellion of 1803") (23 July 1803) and was quickly replaced by Lieutenant-General [Cathcart](/wiki/William_Cathcart,_1st_Earl_Cathcart "William Cathcart, 1st Earl Cathcart"), whose appointment was gazetted on 20 October.

Fox moved to be commander of the London district (1803), [Lieutenant-Governor of Gibraltar](/wiki/Governor_of_Gibraltar "Governor of Gibraltar") (1804–06), Commander-in-Chief in the Mediterranean (1806–07) and [minister to Sicily](/wiki/British_Minister_to_Sicily "British Minister to Sicily"). With his health weakening, Fox passed active command of the force to his deputy, Lieutenant-General Sir [John Moore](/wiki/John_Moore_(British_Army_officer) "John Moore (British Army officer)"). The smallness of his force (made yet smaller when Major-General [Mackenzie Fraser](/wiki/Alexander_Mackenzie-Fraser "Alexander Mackenzie-Fraser") was sent to occupy Alexandria) meant he refused the repeated requests from the Sicilian court and [William Drummond](/wiki/William_Drummond_of_Logiealmond "William Drummond of Logiealmond"), British minister at the Sicilian court, for land operations on the Italian mainland. Fox and Moore also opposed [Royal Navy](/wiki/Royal_Navy "Royal Navy") officer [Sidney Smith](/wiki/Sidney_Smith_(Royal_Navy_officer) "Sidney Smith (Royal Navy officer)")'s political machinations at the Sicilian court, contrary as they were to the army's plans for the Italian theatre, until Fox's ill health finally led to his being recalled by the British government and replaced by Moore. Fox was promoted full general on 25 April 1808, appointed governor of Portsmouth in 1810 and died the following year.

## Marriage and issue

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henry_Edward_Fox&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Marriage and issue")]

On 14 November 1786 he married Marianne Clayton, daughter of William Clayton, [MP](/wiki/Member_of_Parliament "Member of Parliament"), of [Harleyford Manor](/wiki/Harleyford_Manor "Harleyford Manor"), and sister of [William Clayton, 4th Baronet](/wiki/Sir_William_Clayton,_4th_Baronet "Sir William Clayton, 4th Baronet") and [Catherine, Lady Howard de Walden](/wiki/John_Griffin,_4th_Baron_Howard_de_Walden "John Griffin, 4th Baron Howard de Walden"). They had 3 children:

* Louisa Amelia Fox (d. 1828), later wife of Major-General Sir [Henry Bunbury](/wiki/Sir_Henry_Bunbury,_7th_Baronet "Sir Henry Bunbury, 7th Baronet")
* Caroline Amelia Fox (d. 1860), who married Major-General [William Napier](/wiki/William_Francis_Patrick_Napier "William Francis Patrick Napier")
* [Henry Stephen Fox](/wiki/Henry_Stephen_Fox "Henry Stephen Fox") (1791–1846), later the UK's envoy-extraordinary and minister-plenipotentiary to the United States of America
